content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


March 24, 1989


Dwight D. Gentry
Western Elections
Post Office Box 8900
Stockton, CA 95208

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-166

Dear Mr. Gentry:

     This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of Neil Crawford, County Clerk of Solano County, regarding
application of the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act")1/ to the use of the absentee ballot identification
form and attached envelope that you have enclosed with your
request.

QUESTION

     Is the use of the elected county clerk's name on the envelope
attached to the absentee ballot identification form permitted
under the mass mailing restriction of Proposition 73?

CONCLUSION

     The elected county clerk's name may appear on the absentee
ballot envelope, provided the absentee ballot identification form
and attached envelope are standard forms and envelopes of the
agency.




1/ Government Code Section 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Dwight G. Gentry
March 24, 1989
Page 2


FACTS

Western Elections is preparing to print a variety of election
materials for cities and counties throughout the state. You have
requested advice concerning an absentee ballot identification form
and attached envelope that you have enclosed with your request.

The absentee ballot form and envelope are mailed along with
an absentee voter ballot and voting instructions to each voter who
applies for an absentee ballot in Solano County. The form and
envelope are used to return the voting materials to the county
clerk and to identify the voter.

The absentee ballot identification form and attached envelope
will be mailed to more than 200 persons in a calendar month. The
form is a self mailer and includes the name of the county clerk of
Solano County in the mailing address along with the address of the
county clerk's office. The county clerk is an elected officer of
the county.

ANALYSIS

Absentee Ballot Self Mailer

Government Code Section 89001 provides that no newsletter or
other mass mailing shall be sent at public expense. Section
82041.5 defines a mass mailing as more than two hundred
substantially similar pieces of mail sent in a calendar month.
Regulation 18901(c) (copy enclosed) provides that a newsletter or
other mass mailing is prohibited by Section 89001 if the name,
photograph or signature of an elected officer appears on the
document, or if it is designated as being from an elected officer
or his or her office, and the elected officer exercises direction
and control over the content, production, or distribution of the
document.

Regulation 18901 also sets out specific exceptions to the
prohibition on mass mailings. Subsection (e) of the regulation
provides an exception when the name of the elected official appears solely on the standard letterhead, forms or envelopes of the
official's agency. We have said that an absentee ballot application constitutes a form for the purposes of this exception.
(Kurle Advice Letter, No. A-89-099, copy enclosed.) The absentee
ballot identification form also fits within this exception. Thus,
if the absentee ballot identification form is the standard form
used by the agency in elections, the inclusion of the name of an
elected officer is permitted on the form.

We have also said that an agency may include the name of the
elected official "in the standard letterhead ... of the stationary, forms or envelopes of the agency." (Regulation 18901(e)(2);
Harlan Advice Letter, No. A-89-081, copy enclosed.) Here, the

Dwight G. Gentry
March 24, 1989
Page 3


form is a self mailer which includes an envelope. The envelope
has the name of the county clerk one time as part of the address
of the agency to which the form is to be returned. Use of the
county clerk's name in the mailing address space is tantamount to
using it in the return address space. Since the name is used only
once in the standard envelope, we conclude that such use is in
compliance with the restrictions of the Act.

Please be advised, however, that the standard form and
envelope exception and all the other exceptions in Regulation
18901 are to be narrowly construed. Thus, absent use of the name
in the standard letterhead, forms or envelopes of the agency, any
reference to the elected official is prohibited unless it meets
the requirements of another exception.

Applicability

You have also asked about the general applicability of this
advice. You would like to know if you may print other self mail-
ers with substantially the same text in reliance on this advise
letter.

Formal written advice is the application of the law to a
particular set of facts. While the immunity provided by Government Code Section 83114(b) is limited to the requestor and to the
specific facts contained in the formal written advice, this advice
may provide guidance to others in identical factual situations.
(Regulation 18329(b)(7), copy enclosed.)

If I can be of any further assistance to you, please feel
free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
    Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:JWW:plh

Enclosures

K/P GRAPHICS

WESTERN ELECTIONS
Post Office Box 8900
Stockton, California 95208
(209) 465-5614

March 13, 1989


California Fair Political Practices Commission
Attention: John Wallace
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear John,

Enclosed please find a copy of our Form No. 9033, an Identification and
Return Envelope used by the County of Solano. We request your opinion on
whether the printed format of this envelope is in compliance with the
Proposition 73 regulations, specifically regarding the inclusion of the name
of the County Clerk, as highlighted on the sample form. We also request
your decision on the applicability of this opinion: can your opinion
regarding this envelope be applied to other envelopes which include
substantially the same text, as well as the name of an elected County Clerk
as part of the destination address? The County is aware that we are
requesting this opinion as part of our normal preparations for printing.

Usage: This envelope is mailed, along with an absent voter ballot and voting
instructions, to each voter who applies for such an absent voter ballot.
After voting the ballot, the voter encloses the voted ballot in this
envelope and returns the envelope according to the election laws.

If you need additional information about the usage of this form, or if you
have any other questions, please don't hesitate to give us a call. Please
note that the County of Solano has an upcoming election scheduled, for which
these envelopes are required. In order for us to meet legal deadlines for
supplying these envelopes, we must start production on March 28, 1989.
Accordingly, we must have your opinion on this specific form on or before
March 27, 1989. Your decision on applicability would be needed as soon as
possible after that date. We are aware of a number of elections due to
occur shortly after the Solano election that would require the printing of
similar materials, and if you should decide that item-by-item approval is
required, we would need to submit samples of those similar forms.

Sincerely,

Dwight D. Gentry
(209) 465-5614

NOTE: Your Absentee Ballot must
be in the office on or before
Election Day at 8:00 p.m.

I hereby affirm that I am ill or disabled and that I
designate:

_________________________________
(Print name of person returning ballot)

who is my spouse, child, parent, grandparent,
grandchild, brother or sister to return my ballot.

_________________________________
(Signature of Voter)

WARNING: VOTING TWICE CONSTITUTES A CRIME.
In the event that the voter attempts to vote more than
one absent voter's ballot, both ballots will be void.

MARKED OFFICIAL BALLOT ENCLOSED

VOTER: Enclose in this envelope only your voted ballot
and secrecy envelope (if any).

If you have spoiled your ballot and
wish another sent to you, ENCLOSE
the spoiled ballot and mark a check
in this box . . .

[CHECKBOX]

FROM: ADDRESS ON REVERSE SIDE

OPEN CAREFULLY
ABSENT VOTER'S BALLOT ENCLOSED
Form No. 8033 - X/P Western Elections

State of CALIFORNIA, County of SOLANO

I, the undersigned, declare under penalty of perjury that I reside within the
precinct in which I am voting, and that I am the person whose name appears
on this envelope. I herein enclose my ballot in compliance with Division 2,
Chapter 1, of the Elections Code. I have not applied, nor do I intend to apply,
for an absent voter's ballot from any other jurisdiction for the same election.

Your ballot cannot be counted unless you sign here:

_________________________________ _________________________________
Signature                                                    Date

_________________________________ _________________________________
Residence Address in Solano County              City or Town

Postmaster: Deliver to address on other side

•

•

•

IDENTIFICATION AND RETURN ENVELOPE

TO BE OPENED ONLY BY THE CANVASSING BOARD

FOR OFFICE USE ONLY

Pct. No. ___________________________

Party ___________________________

Ballot Style ___________________________

Date Mailed ___________________________

Date Returned ___________________________

Election ___________________________

(Endorse below the cause of challenge
and action thereon or the cause of
rejection):

___________________________

___________________________

___________________________

CANVASSING BOARD

___________________________

___________________________

___________________________

[THREE VERTICAL LINES]

[RECTANGLE BOX]
PLACE
FIRST CLASS
POSTAGE
HERE

TO: NEIL CRAWFORD, COUNTY CLERK /
COUNTY OF SOLANO
POST OFFICE BOX "I"
FAIRFIELD, CA 94533-9800

894-166

requesting this opinion as part of our normal preparations for printing.

Usage: This envelope is mailed, along with an absent voter ballot and voting
instructions, to each voter who applies for such an absent voter ballot.
After voting the ballot, the voter encloses the voted ballot in this
envelope and returns the envelope according to the election laws.

If you need additional information about the usage of this form, or if you
have any other questions, please don't hesitate to give us a call. Please
note that the County of Solano has an upcoming election scheduled, for which
these envelopes are required. In order for us to meet legal deadlines for

Accordingly, we must have your opinion on this specific form on or before

Sincerely,

Dwight D. Gentry
(209) 465-5614

NOTE: Your Absentee Ballot must
be in the office on or before

and secrecy envelope (if any).

If you have spoiled your ballot and

in this box . . .

State of CALIFORNIA, County of SOLANO

I, the undersigned, declare under penalty of perjury that I reside within the

Your ballot cannot be counted unless you sign here:

✓ _________________________     _________________
    Signature                        Date

    Residence Address in Solano County     City or Town

TO BE OPENED ONLY BY THE CANVASSING BOARD

FROM: ADDRESS ON REVERSE SIDE
OPEN CAREFULLY

Form No. 3033 - K/P Western Elections

FOR OFFICE USE ONLY

Pct. No. _________________________

Party _________________________

Ballot Style _________________________

Date Mailed _________________________

and action thereon or the cause of

                                          PLACE
                                      FIRST CLASS
                                       POSTAGE
                                         HERE


TO: NEIL CRAWFORD, COUNTY CLERK /
    COUNTY OF SOLANO
    POST OFFICE BOX "I"
    FAIRFIELD, CA 94533-0600

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 16, 1989

Dwight D. Gentry
K/P Graphics
Western Elections
P.O. Box 8900
Stockton, CA  95208

Re:  Letter No. 89-166

Dear Mr. Gentry:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on March 15, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact John Wallace an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329.)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel
